docs.title: omnipath-client
docs.text:
Python client for the OmniPath molecular biology prior-knowledge web API.

omnipath-client provides validated, cache-aware access to the OmniPath API, delivering results as polars, pandas, or pyarrow DataFrames. Network data can optionally be returned as annnet graph objects.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Export endpoints for entities, interactions, and associations (complexes, pathways, reactions)
  • Ontology endpoints for term lookup, search, and hierarchy trees
  • Multi-backend DataFrame output: polars (default), pandas, pyarrow
  • Query validation against the API schema (endpoint names, parameter names, enum values)
  • Caching of downloaded data via download-manager
  • Graph conversion to annnet objects for network data

docs.title: Quick example
docs.text:
import omnipath_client as op

# Get all interactions as a polars DataFrame
df = op.interactions()

# Filter by direction
df = op.interactions(direction='directed')

# Get entities for a specific organism
df = op.entities(taxonomy_ids=['9606'])

# Return interactions as an annnet graph
g = op.interactions(as_graph=True)

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text:
• Installation -- how to install the package
  • Quickstart -- basic usage examples

docs.title: About
docs.text: omnipath-client is developed by the Saez-Rodriguez group at Heidelberg University Hospital.

docs.title: Data licensing
docs.text: The data served by OmniPath is combined from many original resources, each with their own license terms. The OmniPath client software is BSD-3-Clause, but the data is subject to the licenses of the original sources. Some resources restrict commercial use. Tools and documentation for managing license-based access control will be provided in a future release.

docs.title: License
docs.text: The client software is licensed under BSD-3-Clause. See the LICENSE file for details.

docs.title: Citation
docs.text:
If you use OmniPath in your research, please cite:

Türei D, Schaul J, Palacio-Escat N, Bohár B, Bai Y, Ceccarelli F, Çevrim E, Daley M, Darcan M, Dimitrov D, Doğan T, Domingo-Fernández D, Dugourd A, Gábor A, Gul L, Hall BA, Hoyt CT, Ivanova O, Klein M, Lawrence T, Mañanes D, Módos D, Müller-Dott S, Ölbei M, Schmidt C, Şen B, Theis FJ, Ünlü A, Ulusoy E, Valdeolivas A, Korcsmáros T, Saez-Rodriguez J. (2026) OmniPath: integrated knowledgebase for multi-omics analysis. Nucleic Acids Research 54(D1):D652-D660. doi:10.1093/nar/gkaf1126

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: • Python >= 3.10

docs.title: Install with pip
docs.text: pip install omnipath-client

docs.title: Install from source
docs.text:
pip install git+https://github.com/saezlab/omnipath-client.git

Or for development:

git clone https://github.com/saezlab/omnipath-client.git
cd omnipath-client
pip install -e ".[dev,tests]"

docs.title: Optional dependencies
docs.text:
omnipath-client supports multiple DataFrame backends. The default backend is polars, which must be installed separately:

pip install polars

For other backends:

pip install pandas    # pandas backend
pip install pyarrow   # pyarrow backend (also required for Parquet reading)

For graph conversion to annnet objects:

pip install annnet

docs.title: Using uv
docs.text:
If you use uv for dependency management:

uv add omnipath-client
uv add polars  # or pandas, pyarrow

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: This guide covers the basic usage of omnipath-client.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text: import omnipath_client as op

docs.title: Entities
docs.text:
Entities include proteins, complexes, and other molecular species:

# All entities
df = op.entities()

# Filter by organism (NCBI taxonomy ID 9606 = human)
df = op.entities(taxonomy_ids=['9606'])

# Filter by entity type
df = op.entities(entity_types=['protein'])

docs.title: Interactions
docs.text:
Interactions represent relationships between entities:

# All interactions
df = op.interactions()

# Directed interactions only
df = op.interactions(direction='directed')

# Filter by sign (activation/inhibition)
df = op.interactions(sign='positive')

# Filter by source database
df = op.interactions(sources=['SIGNOR'])

docs.title: Associations
docs.text:
Associations represent membership in complexes, pathways, and reactions:

# All associations
df = op.associations()

# Filter by parent entity type (e.g. complexes)
df = op.associations(parent_entity_types=['complex'])

docs.title: Choosing a DataFrame backend
docs.text:
By default, results are returned as polars DataFrames. You can choose a different backend:

# pandas DataFrame
df = op.entities(backend='pandas')

# pyarrow Table
table = op.entities(backend='pyarrow')

To set the default backend for all queries, use the OO client:

client = op.OmniPath(backend='pandas')
df = client.entities()

docs.title: Graph conversion
docs.text:
Interactions and associations can be returned as annnet graph objects:

# Interactions as a graph
g = op.interactions(as_graph=True)

# Associations as a graph
g = op.associations(as_graph=True)

docs.title: Ontology endpoints
docs.text:
Look up ontology terms, search by name, or build hierarchy trees:

# Batch term lookup
result = op.ontology_terms(['GO:0006915', 'MI:0326'])

# Search terms by name
result = op.search_terms(['apoptosis', 'kinase'])

# Get a merged hierarchy tree
tree = op.ontology_tree(['GO:0006915'])

# List available ontologies
ontologies = op.ontologies()

docs.title: Introspection
docs.text:
Explore available endpoints, parameters, and allowed values:

client = op.OmniPath()

# All endpoints
client.endpoint_registry

# Parameters for an endpoint
client.params('exports/interactions/parquet')

# Allowed values for a parameter
client.values('exports/interactions/parquet', 'direction')
# ['any', 'directed', 'undirected']

docs.title: Custom API URL
docs.text:
To use a different API server (e.g. a local instance):

client = op.OmniPath(base_url='http://localhost:8081')
df = client.interactions()

docs.title: Client
docs.text:
Client for the OmniPath web API.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default base_url str

Base URL of the OmniPath API.

DEFAULT_BASE_URL backend BackendType

Default DataFrame backend.

'auto' cache bool

Whether to cache downloaded files.

True

docs.title: endpoint_registry property
docs.text: All registered endpoints.

docs.title: entities(backend=None, **filters)
docs.text:
Export entities.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default backend BackendType | None

Override the default DataFrame backend.

None **filters Any

Filter parameters (entity_ids, entity_types, sources, taxonomy_ids, etc.).

{}

Returns:

Type Description Any

A DataFrame of entities.

docs.title: interactions(as_graph=False, backend=None, **filters)
docs.text:
Export interactions.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default as_graph bool

If True, return an annnet.Graph instead of a DataFrame.

False backend BackendType | None

Override the default DataFrame backend.

None **filters Any

Filter parameters (entity_ids, interaction_types, direction, sign, etc.).

{}

Returns:

Type Description Any

A DataFrame of interactions, or an annnet.Graph.

docs.title: associations(as_graph=False, backend=None, **filters)
docs.text:
Export associations (complexes, pathways, reactions).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default as_graph bool

If True, return an annnet.Graph instead of a DataFrame.

False backend BackendType | None

Override the default DataFrame backend.

None **filters Any

Filter parameters (parent_entity_ids, member_entity_ids, etc.).

{}

Returns:

Type Description Any

A DataFrame of associations, or an annnet.Graph.

docs.title: ontology_terms(term_ids)
docs.text:
Batch lookup of ontology terms.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default term_ids list[str]

List of term IDs (e.g. ['GO:0006915']).

required

Returns:

Type Description Any

A dict with term information.

docs.title: ontology_tree(term_ids)
docs.text:
Get merged hierarchy tree for terms.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default term_ids list[str]

List of term IDs.

required

Returns:

Type Description Any

A tree structure dict.

docs.title: search_terms(queries, limit=10)
docs.text:
Search ontology terms by name or synonym.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default queries list[str]

Search strings.

required limit int

Maximum number of results per query.

10

Returns:

Type Description Any

A dict with search results.

docs.title: ontologies()
docs.text:
List all available ontologies.

Returns:

Type Description Any

A dict with ontology information.

docs.title: interaction_evidence(interaction_id)
docs.text:
Get full evidence for a single interaction.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default interaction_id int

The interaction ID.

required

Returns:

Type Description Any

Evidence data as a dict.

docs.title: association_evidence(association_id)
docs.text:
Get full evidence for a single association.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default association_id int

The association ID.

required

Returns:

Type Description Any

Evidence data as a dict.

docs.title: Response parsing
docs.text:
Parse an API response and convert to the requested backend.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default source str | Path | BytesIO

Path to a file or an in-memory buffer.

required response_format ResponseFormat

The format of the response data.

'parquet' backend BackendType

The target DataFrame backend. Use 'auto' to select the first available backend (tries polars, pandas, pyarrow in order).

'auto'

Returns:

Type Description Any

A DataFrame in the requested backend format.

docs.title: Graph conversion
docs.text:
Convert an interactions DataFrame to an annnet Graph.

Maps interaction columns to annnet edge format: member_a_id -> source, member_b_id -> target.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default df Any

An interactions DataFrame (any backend).

required

Returns:

Type Description Any

An annnet.Graph instance.

Convert an associations DataFrame to an annnet Graph.

Associations represent parent-member relationships (complexes, pathways, reactions).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default df Any

An associations DataFrame (any backend).

required

Returns:

Type Description Any

An annnet.Graph instance.

docs.title: Inventory
docs.text:
Registry of all known API endpoints and parameters.

Fetches the OpenAPI schema from the server and parses it into endpoint definitions. Falls back to static definitions on failure.

docs.title: endpoints property
docs.text: All registered endpoints.

docs.title: load(force_refresh=False)
docs.text:
Load the inventory from the server or static fallback.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default force_refresh bool

If True, bypass any cached inventory and re-fetch.

False

docs.title: params(endpoint)
docs.text:
Parameters for a given endpoint.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default endpoint str

The endpoint key, e.g. 'exports/interactions/parquet'.

required

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, ParamDef]

A dict mapping parameter names to ParamDef instances.

docs.title: allowed_values(endpoint, param)
docs.text:
Allowed values for a parameter on an endpoint.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default endpoint str

The endpoint key.

required param str

The parameter name.

required

Returns:

Type Description list[str] | None

A list of allowed values, or None if unconstrained.

docs.title: Query
docs.text:
Builds and validates queries against the API inventory.

A validated query for an OmniPath API endpoint.

docs.title: build(endpoint, **params)
docs.text:
Build a validated query.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default endpoint str

The endpoint key, e.g. 'exports/interactions/parquet'.

required **params Any

Query parameters.

{}

Returns:

Type Description Query

A validated Query instance.

Raises:

Type Description UnknownEndpointError

If the endpoint is not in the inventory.

UnknownParameterError

If a parameter is not recognized.

InvalidParameterValueError

If a value is not in the allowed set.

docs.title: url property
docs.text: Full URL for this query.

docs.title: json_body property
docs.text:
JSON request body for POST endpoints.

Returns None for GET endpoints.

docs.title: query_params property
docs.text:
URL query parameters for GET endpoints.

Returns an empty dict for POST endpoints.

docs.title: path_params property
docs.text: Path parameters extracted from the query params.

docs.title: resolved_url property
docs.text: URL with path parameters substituted.

docs.title: Data classes
docs.text:
Definition of a single API endpoint.

Definition of a single endpoint parameter.

docs.title: Exceptions
docs.text: Exception hierarchy for omnipath-client.

docs.title: OmniPathError
docs.text:
Bases: Exception

Base exception for all omnipath-client errors.

docs.title: OmniPathAPIError
docs.text:
Bases: OmniPathError

Error returned by the OmniPath API (HTTP 4xx/5xx).

docs.title: OmniPathConnectionError
docs.text:
Bases: OmniPathError

Network or connection error when contacting the API.

docs.title: QueryValidationError
docs.text:
Bases: OmniPathError

Base for query parameter validation errors.

docs.title: UnknownEndpointError
docs.text:
Bases: QueryValidationError

The requested endpoint is not in the inventory.

docs.title: UnknownParameterError
docs.text:
Bases: QueryValidationError

A parameter name is not recognized for the endpoint.

docs.title: InvalidParameterValueError
docs.text:
Bases: QueryValidationError

A parameter value is not in the set of allowed values.

docs.title: MissingParameterError
docs.text:
Bases: QueryValidationError

A required parameter was not provided.

docs.title: BackendNotAvailableError
docs.text:
Bases: OmniPathError

The requested DataFrame backend is not installed.
